name: FDD Scope Mining — Phase 2 (Industry Agents) + Phase 3 (Synthesis)

userStories.title: Mine Construction industry SOW
userStories.description:
Read all extracted JSON files from dist/el-samples-text/Construction/ and produce a structured FDD scope catalog.

Role: You are a Financial Due Diligence (FDD) scope analyst.

Context: We are building a custom GPT that generates Statements of Work for KPMG Transaction Services. The GPT needs to produce industry-specific FDD scope sections deterministically. To do this, we are mining real SOW documents to build a structured library of scope content organized by section and industry.

Your industry: Construction

Your files (structured JSON with paragraph text + formatting metadata):
- dist/el-samples-text/Construction/Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json

The JSON files contain paragraph objects with: text, style (Word style name), bold (boolean), indent_level (0=heading/body, 1+=bullet), font_size_pt. Use these signals to identify headings vs bullets vs body text.

Your task: Read every file and produce a structured JSON catalog. Extract:
1. Scope period statement — fiscal years and trailing periods covered
2. Phases — if split into phases (pre-bid, post-bid), note each
3. Sections — every major section heading (Business overview, Quality of earnings, Revenue analysis, Operating expenses, Working capital, AR, AP, Net debt, Capex, etc.)
4. Sub-bullets per section — specific procedures/analyses listed under each section. Copy verbatim.
5. Industry-specific language — flag terms specific to Construction (e.g., project-based revenue, retention holdbacks, change orders, bonding, subcontractor arrangements)
6. Workstream identification — if multiple workstreams exist (financial DD, tax DD, IT DD), note which sections belong where. Focus on financial DD only.

Output: Write JSON to docs/mining/construction.json with structure: { industry, files_analyzed, scope_periods, phases, sections (each with heading, normalized_heading, appears_in, is_industry_specific, bullets[]), industry_specific_terms, workstreams_found, notes }

Rules: Copy bullet text verbatim. Normalize headings to snake_case. Only extract FDD content. If a file has minimal content, note it.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/mining/construction.json exists and is valid JSON
JSON contains industry field set to 'Construction'
JSON contains files_analyzed listing all input files
JSON contains sections array with heading, normalized_heading, appears_in, is_industry_specific, and bullets for each section found
JSON contains industry_specific_terms array
JSON contains workstreams_found array
Bullet text is copied verbatim from source (not summarized or rewritten)
userStories.verificationCommand: python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('docs/mining/construction.json')); assert d['industry']=='Construction'; assert 'sections' in d; assert len(d['sections'])>0; print('PASS')"
files: dist/el-samples-text/Construction/Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json

userStories.title: Mine Eyecare industry SOWs
userStories.description:
Read all extracted JSON files from dist/el-samples-text/Eyecare/ and produce a structured FDD scope catalog.

Role: You are a Financial Due Diligence (FDD) scope analyst.

Context: We are building a custom GPT that generates Statements of Work for KPMG Transaction Services. The GPT needs to produce industry-specific FDD scope sections deterministically. To do this, we are mining real SOW documents to build a structured library of scope content organized by section and industry.

Your industry: Eyecare

Your files (structured JSON with paragraph text + formatting metadata):
- dist/el-samples-text/Eyecare/Axis - Eng Procedures.json
- dist/el-samples-text/Eyecare/Project View - SOW 2022-04-12.json

The JSON files contain paragraph objects with: text, style (Word style name), bold (boolean), indent_level (0=heading/body, 1+=bullet), font_size_pt. Use these signals to identify headings vs bullets vs body text.

Your task: Read every file and produce a structured JSON catalog. Extract:
1. Scope period statement
2. Phases
3. Sections — every major section heading
4. Sub-bullets per section — copy verbatim
5. Industry-specific language — flag terms specific to Eyecare (e.g., optometry clinics, lens/frame revenue, patient visits, insurance reimbursement)
6. Workstream identification

Output: Write JSON to docs/mining/eyecare.json

Rules: Copy bullet text verbatim. Normalize headings to snake_case. Only extract FDD content. If two files have the same section, include bullets from BOTH and tag each with its source.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/mining/eyecare.json exists and is valid JSON
JSON contains industry field set to 'Eyecare'
JSON contains files_analyzed listing all input files
JSON contains sections array with heading, normalized_heading, appears_in, is_industry_specific, and bullets for each section found
JSON contains industry_specific_terms array
JSON contains workstreams_found array
Bullet text is copied verbatim from source (not summarized or rewritten)
Where both files have the same section, bullets from both are included with source tags

userStories.title: Mine Manufacturing industry SOWs
userStories.description:
Read all extracted JSON files from dist/el-samples-text/Manufacturing/ and produce a structured FDD scope catalog.

Role: You are a Financial Due Diligence (FDD) scope analyst.

Your industry: Manufacturing

- dist/el-samples-text/Manufacturing/Project Diamond - Draft SOW (02-Jul-24) vSENT.json
- dist/el-samples-text/Manufacturing/Project Sourdough - KPMG scope - 28-Aug-2024 v2.json

Note: EL Flag.json is extracted from a PDF and may contain very little text. If so, note it as 'insufficient content' in notes.

The JSON files contain paragraph objects with: text, style, bold, indent_level, font_size_pt.

Extract: scope periods, phases, sections with verbatim bullets, industry-specific terms (e.g., production capacity, utilization, scrap rates, raw material pricing, VDD Report review, constant currency analysis, inventory valuation, costing methodology), workstreams (Manufacturing SOWs often include tax DD, IT DD, HR DD alongside financial DD).

Output: docs/mining/manufacturing.json

Rules: Verbatim bullets. snake_case headings. FDD content only. Include bullets from all files with source tags.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/mining/manufacturing.json exists and is valid JSON
JSON contains industry field set to 'Manufacturing'
JSON contains files_analyzed listing all input files
JSON contains sections array with heading, normalized_heading, appears_in, is_industry_specific, and bullets for each section found
JSON contains industry_specific_terms array

Bullet text is copied verbatim from source
EL Flag.json is noted in files_analyzed with a note if content was insufficient
userStories.verificationCommand: python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('docs/mining/manufacturing.json')); assert d['industry']=='Manufacturing'; assert 'sections' in d; assert len(d['sections'])>0; print('PASS')"

userStories.title: Synthesize mining outputs into unified FDD scope library
userStories.description:
Read all 11 mining output JSON files from docs/mining/ and produce a unified FDD scope library.

Role: You are a Financial Due Diligence (FDD) scope librarian.

Context: 11 industry mining agents have each produced a structured JSON catalog (docs/mining/*.json) containing sections, bullets, and industry-specific terms from real SOW documents. Your job is to synthesize these into a single unified library.

Your task:
1. Read all 11 JSON files from docs/mining/
2. Identify the COMMON SKELETON — sections that appear in 8+ of 11 industries. For each common section, select the most representative/complete default bullets (these will be the starting point for all industries).
3. Identify INDUSTRY-SPECIFIC MODULES — for each industry, collect bullets/sections that are unique to that vertical or that extend the common skeleton with industry-specific detail.
4. Produce a unified library file: reference/fdd_scope_library.json

Output structure:
{
  "common_skeleton": [
    {
      "heading": "Business overview",
      "normalized_heading": "business_overview",
      "default_bullets": ["...", "..."]
    }
  ],
  "industry_modules": {
    "construction": {
      "business_overview": ["industry-specific bullet", ...],
      "quality_of_earnings": ["...", ...]
    },
    "healthcare": { ... },
    ...
  },
  "metadata": {
    "industries_analyzed": 11,
    "common_threshold": "8 of 11",
    "total_sections_found": N,
    "total_bullets_cataloged": N
  }
}

Rules:
- A section is 'common' if it appears in 8+ industries
- Default bullets for common sections should be the most generic/representative wording (not industry-specific)
- Industry modules only contain bullets that ADD to or REPLACE common skeleton bullets
- Preserve verbatim bullet text from the mining outputs
acceptanceCriteria: reference/fdd_scope_library.json exists and is valid JSON
JSON contains common_skeleton array with sections that appear in 8+ industries
Each common_skeleton entry has heading, normalized_heading, and default_bullets
JSON contains industry_modules object with one key per industry
Each industry module maps normalized_heading to industry-specific bullets
JSON contains metadata with industries_analyzed, common_threshold, total_sections_found, total_bullets_cataloged
All 11 industry mining outputs were read and incorporated
userStories.verificationCommand: python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('reference/fdd_scope_library.json')); assert 'common_skeleton' in d; assert len(d['common_skeleton'])>0; assert 'industry_modules' in d; assert len(d['industry_modules'])>=8; assert 'metadata' in d; print('PASS')"
